source: apify/instagram-scraper (own account: manual fallback — Instagram blocks scraper access to this small account)

own.bio: Strange but true stories, told quietly. New odd hour, every week.

posts.caption: In 2021, one engineer typed one wrong command and 3.5 billion people lost the internet in a single instant. Not hacked. Not attacked. Just one wrong line of code during a routine maintenance check. Facebook's own engineers couldn't even log in to fix it, their access tools were on the same servers that just vanished. They had to physically drive to data centers and override servers by hand. One by one. 6 hours. $60 million lost every single hour. And the exact same vulnerability? Still exists right now. The internet is more fragile than you think.

2021 mein ek engineer ne ek galat command type ki aur 3.5 billion logon ka internet ek pal mein band ho gaya. Hack nahi hua. Attack nahi hua. Bas ek routine maintenance check ke dauran ek galat line of code. 6 ghante. Har ghante 60 million dollar ka nuksaan. Internet utna strong nahi jitna hum sochte hain.

There is a sound that only 2% of people can hear. Scientists have studied it for 50 years and still have no answer. It's called The Hum — and the leading theory is that it's the Earth itself vibrating at a frequency most humans aren't built to detect. A woman heard it for 11 years, moved across the country to escape it, and it followed her to the new house. Stop right now, close your eyes, and listen. What do you hear?

We call space the final frontier. But we've barely explored our own planet. Earth is stranger than anything we've found out there — and most people have no idea. From perfect solar eclipses to oceans deeper than mountains are tall, this is the planet you thought you knew.

In 1974, a man calculated the exact day he would die and spent a year preparing for it. What happened next is stranger than the prediction itself. Frank was obsessed with numerology. He told his family, quit his job, and gave away everything he owned. When the day came and went without incident, he thought he'd beaten fate. He hadn't.

They had to physically drive to data centers and override servers by hand. One by one. 6 hours. $60 million lost every single hour. And the exact same vulnerability? Still exists right now. The internet is more fragile than you think.

competitors.bio:
Sciency videos made with love, a lot of time and after effects.
Links to our videos, shop, website & more:

recentPosts.caption: Caption this Nightshift scene
Yesterday we talked about the top 3 microbe predators, and today we share the top 3 memes from that video 😌

There is a hidden world filled with monsters you never heard of, from predators who spit acid and shoot harpoons, to armored hunters that devour their prey alive! Deep in the oceans, and even in fresh water from puddles and lakes, these microscopic super predators remained hidden in plain sight, and evolved over the last 2 billion years.

What are the deadliest predators of the microworld, and what kind of bizarre biological weapons have they evolved over the past billions of years? Watch our fu
When someone opens snacks in the office
What could 1% of the richest countries’ income achieve? From malaria vaccines and clean water to renewable energy, a surprisingly small fraction of global wealth could transform millions of lives.

Your favorite shirt of the summer now comes with a very special souvenir from space!

Get a free set of Space Tour Patches when you buy a Space Tour Shirt, this summer* ✨
We teamed up with artist @haxloeffler to create this exclusive, two-sided shirt design featuring a cosmic throwback to our most iconic space videos. Suit up, secure your free patches, and browse a few more cosmic essentials for the journey.

*Starting from 23.06.2026 until 07.07.2026, when you purchase the Space Tour Shirt in t

Could YOU survive a royal marriage? Henry VIII had six wives, but becoming Queen of England is only the beginning of the challenge. To survive this, you have to navigate religious upheaval, political games, shifting alliances, and the unpredictable moods of one of history's most notorious monarchs. Some lost their crowns, and some lost their lives, but only one outlived the king.

Who were Henry VIII's six wives, and why was marrying him a death sentence for most of them? And what about you, cou
Yep, it do be like that sometimes... 💀

This is the deadliest virus on our planet… However, human cells are safe. The deadliest virus on our planet only feasts on bacteria, hence its name: Bacteriophage - bacteria eater. Now that our antibiotics aren't as effective against the ever-evolving multiresistant bacteria, phages are a promising tool for defending ourselves in the future. They are highly specialized so they only attack the bacteria they’re supposed to, reducing the side effects of a treatment.

If the Moon tasted like cheese, we are sure this asteroid would taste like an orange-flavored jellybean!

Have you already started your space adventure? Star Birds, which we developed together with our friends at Toukana Interactive, is available on Steam!

Which year of your life is the most dangerous? For many people, it’s not old age but infancy. Where you're born can dramatically change your chances of survival, and millions of child deaths are still preventable today.

Behind every one of our videos is a producer keeping hundreds of moving pieces in orbit.
Now we're looking for two more. ✨
If you want to work with a team that's making science and history accessible and inspiring curiosity for millions of people around the world, check out our open Producer roles. 
Link in Bio 💌

competitors.bio: Discover the most incredible experiences from around the world 🌎✈️ Business: info@damiermedia.com

recentPosts.caption:
This Shark-Free Lagoon looks like a perfect tropical escape,  but what makes it even more fascinating is ‘what you don’t find there’.⁠
⁠
Despite being part of the Red Sea region, this lagoon is completely safe from sharks due to its extreme salinity and shallow depth. The water is also unusually warm, creating a calm, almost surreal swimming environment.⁠
⁠
It’s one of those rare places where nature quietly creates a space that feels both beautiful and unusual at the same time.⁠
⁠
By: Matriki Be

Lake Natron in Tanzania is one of the most extreme environments on Earth.⁠
⁠
Its waters are highly alkaline and rich in minerals, giving the lake its striking red color and creating conditions so harsh that most life cannot survive.⁠
⁠
In some cases, animals that come into contact with the lake can become naturally preserved by the minerals, taking on a stone-like appearance over time.⁠
⁠
Yet despite its harshness, Lake Natron is also a crucial breeding ground for thousands of flamingos, who thr

In Japan, one of the most expensive coffees in the world is so exclusive that even a single drop can cost around $20.⁠
⁠
The beans are carefully sourced and processed in extremely limited quantities, so every cup feels more like a luxury experience than a daily drink.⁠
⁠
What makes it even more surprising is that despite the price, it attracts coffee lovers from around the world who are curious to try something this rare.⁠
⁠
Would you try this special coffee?⁠
⁠
By: @tripadvisor (GINA, juneshun)

Red pandas may look like tiny bears, but they’re actually more closely related to raccoons than giant pandas. Native to the forests of the Himalayas and southwestern China, they spend most of their time in trees and feed mainly on bamboo.

📷 by @rustamnazimov13

Inside Microsoft's headquarters is a room so quiet that silence itself becomes uncomfortable.⁠
⁠
The anechoic chamber in Redmond, Washington, absorbs almost every sound, reaching an astonishing -20.35 decibels, making it the quietest place on Earth.⁠
⁠
People who go inside can hear things they normally never notice: their heartbeat, breathing, and even the sounds coming from inside their own bodies.⁠
⁠
By: @microsoft @guinnessworldrecords @cnn

Africa’s largest airport is currently being built in Ethiopia, a massive $12.5 billion project set to transform aviation on the continent.⁠
⁠
Located near Bishoftu, the new hub is designed to handle millions of passengers annually, significantly expanding international connectivity and positioning Ethiopia as a key gateway between Africa and the rest of the world.⁠
⁠
Beyond its scale, the project reflects the country’s long-term investment in infrastructure, trade, and tourism growth.⁠
⁠
By: @cn

“Paul the Octopus,” rose to global fame during the 2010 FIFA World Cup after correctly “predicting” several match outcomes by choosing between food boxes marked with different flags.⁠
⁠
Millions watched each decision as it turned into a viral moment around the world.⁠
⁠
While it appeared to have an extraordinary ability, experts later explained it was most likely a coincidence.⁠
⁠
Paul has since passed away, but his legacy remains one of the most unusual stories in sports history.⁠
⁠
By: @wired

Some lakes on Earth don’t look real at all.⁠
⁠
From neon pink waters filled with salt-loving bacteria to deep volcanic lakes that change color depending on the light, these places feel like they belong on another planet rather than our own.⁠
⁠
Nature has created landscapes so surreal that at first glance, you almost don’t believe they’re real.⁠
⁠
By: @refoorest @fishnfins_palau @newsweek @followalice.travel @mohdatirazmi @geologyscience

You can watch Jaws while floating in the water in Texas.⁠
⁠
In Austin, a floating cinema experience turns classic movie nights into something completely different, with viewers relaxing on inflatable rafts as films play on a giant outdoor screen.⁠
⁠
Watching a shark movie while surrounded by water adds a whole new layer to the experience, making it both fun and thrilling at the same time.⁠
⁠
By: @drafthouse

This is what Mexican water-tubing looks like 
📷 by @huastecapotosina_rutaturquesa

Meet Merlin, a duck from Mexico City who somehow ended up becoming part of the World Cup story in the most unexpected way.⁠
⁠
He was already known around the neighborhood, often seen walking through the streets with his owner, but everything changed when videos of him wearing a tiny Mexico jersey during World Cup celebrations started circulating online.⁠
⁠
From there, people began recognizing him, stopping for photos, and sharing clips of him as he moved through the city.⁠
⁠
By: @mexiconewsdaily

China built a skyscraper the length of three football fields.⁠
⁠
In Chongqing, a 300-meter-long glass structure connects four skyscrapers and sits more than 250 meters above the ground.⁠
⁠
Instead of expanding outward or upward, engineers built across the skyline, creating one of the most unusual architectural projects in the world.⁠
⁠
By: @safdiearchitects @metalocus @capitaland

competitors.bio: Step into wonder and find your inner explorer with National Geographic 🌎

recentPosts.caption:
There's more to the story of Pompeii than just destruction. Join Tom Hiddleston as he traces the stories of those who experienced the eruption and those who survived.

#PompeiiOutOfTime with @twhiddleston premieres July 22 on @NatGeoTV. Streaming on @DisneyPlus and @hulu.

@antoni is on a quest to find the best sights, tastes, and experiences the world has to offer, and you're invited. 

#BestOfTheWorld with Antoni Porowski premieres Sunday, June 7 at 9/8c on National Geographic. Stream on @DisneyPlus and @hulu

When archaeologists excavated a medieval toilet in Germany, they uncovered a 700-year-old reusable wax notebook. Likened to an "Etch A Sketch," wax page journals could be erased using heat from a fire. Kept surprisingly intact by a sealed leather pouch, researchers say the Latin inscriptions could shed new light on life in medieval Germany. 

Learn more about the journal and what it says at the link in bio. 

Photograph courtesy of LWL-Archaeology for Westphalia/E. Daood

In 2025, Kazi and 15 other cheetahs undertook a grueling 53-hour translocation from South Africa to Mozambique—the largest cheetah relocation ever attempted. Such a journey can be risky, but it's necessary to ensure the survival of a species with fewer than 7,100 individuals remaining worldwide, both in the wild and in captivity. After navigating bureaucracy, torrential rain, and a car crash, the effort has proven initially successful one year on, with Kazi giving birth to these three cubs.

Fol

Some sharks don't have to constantly be on the move, and the result is behavior that's rare to see.

#HammerheadSharksUpClose with @bertiegregory is now streaming on @DisneyPlus and @hulu, part of #Sharkfest on National Geographic.

Living on the steepest street in the continental United States comes with some uphill battles—from icy winter commutes to runaway trash cans.
 
Michael Hartman, a certified first responder who has lived on Canton Avenue, in Pittsburgh's Beechview neighborhood, for five years, has been known to administer first aid to the cyclists who regularly test their mettle on the climb during an annual race.
 
With a maximum grade of 37 percent, Canton is not the kind of street you leisurely stroll—or bike—

Everyone loves hammerhead sharks. Humans, demigods, everyone.

#HammerheadSharksUpClose with Bertie Gregory is now streaming on @DisneyPlus and @hulu, part of #Sharkfest on National Geographic.

And don’t miss #Moana, arriving only in theaters this Friday.
Ready to go back in time? Two weeks until Pompeii: Out of Time with Tom Hiddleston premieres on @natgeotv

Some of the Bible's best-known stories are gaining new historical context as archaeologists uncover long-hidden evidence. Across ancient cities, early Christian sites, and centuries-old manuscripts—including recovered traces of the Apostle Paul's letters—six new discoveries shed light on early church history.

Learn more about the latest archaeological breakthroughs at the link in bio. 

Photograph by Khalil Hamra/Ap Photo

Several Hawaiian spots contend for the title of rainiest place, but Mount Waiʻaleʻale on the island of Kauaʻi has set eye-popping records, topping a hundred inches in a month as recently as 2021. The nearly mile-high peak snags moist trade winds, which cool and condense around its summit, enrobing the mountain in waterfalls.
 
In recognition of the 250th anniversary of U.S. independence, we're celebrating the grandest, wildest, and strangest bits of American geography and culture across every st

Would you swim with sharks like @bertiegregory? 🦈

#HammerheadSharksUpClose with Bertie Gregory is now streaming on @DisneyPlus and @hulu, part of #Sharkfest on National Geographic.

Photos by @joelsartore | Meet the giant snake-necked turtle, the largest of the long-necked turtles found in Australia. Turtles like this one play an important role in river ecosystems, especially after a fish kill. They will feed on dead fish, returning ammonia and dissolved oxygen back into the river in just three to six days (natural decomposition can take nearly a month). These photos were taken at @tennesseeaquarium. To see more species featured in the Photo Ark, follow @joelsartore. #Photo

competitors.bio:
A window to our world 🌎
This is a commercial channel from BBC Studios.

recentPosts.caption:
Retiring at 100 years old? Not a chance! Here’s a sneak peek of Sir David Attenborough working on the narration for the upcoming BLUE PLANET III.

#DavidAttenborough100 #DavidAttenborough #BluePlanetIII

One of nature’s greatest acts of partnership 🪺
 
To give their chicks the best possible start, a female hornbill walls herself inside a tree hollow for months. With only a tiny opening between the, her survival and that of her family rests entirely in her partner’s care. 
 
Celebrate 20 years of Planet Earth with us. Find out where to watch the full episode via our link in bio 💚

#PlanetEarth #DavidAttenborough #PE20 #PEIII

Beware the ant army 🐜 

To consume prey, ants will swarm insects like crickets and caterpillars using sheer force of numbers. They can even overwhelm wounded chickens, goats and pigs. Nothing is off the menu… including @chrisgpackham2!

Find out where to watch Evolution via the link in our bio 🔗💚

#Evolution #ChrisPackham #Nature

You’ll never look at a tennis player’s racket arm the same way again 🎾
 
As a professional tennis player trains year after year, their skeleton adapts to the repeated impact of every shot. This causes the bones in their racket arm to become up to 20% thicker and stronger than the other and reduces the risk of breaking.
 
Watch the full clip and other videos from Secrets of the Human Body via the link in bio 🔗
 
#Tennis #Wimbeldon #RacketSport

Evolution: Official Trailer 
 
Uncover the 4-billion-year story behind five iconic animals, from their simple origins to the complex creatures we see today. Chris Packham embarks on a series of adventures through deep time, revealing how evolution shaped them in the most surprising ways.
 
Find out where to watch via the link in our bio 🔗💚

#Evolution #ChrisPackham #Nature

How did life on Earth become what it is today? 
 
Join Chris Packham on a spectacular journey through four billion years of evolution. From familiar creatures to unimaginable ancestors, this series explores simple yet powerful questions, revealing the forces that have shaped life on our planet as we know it today. 
 
Trailer drops tomorrow! 
 
#Evolution #ChrisPackham

That was a close call 🦩
 
Flamingo chicks face one of their first survival tests when the lake freezes overnight. While the adults have the strength to break free, many of the young birds are left stranded. Watch how an unexpected shift in the wind offers the remaining chicks a chance to break free and continue their journey.
 
Explore the world’s few remaining untouched landscapes with Eden: Untamed Planet. Find the full series via the link in our bio 🔗
 
#EdenUntamedPlanet #Flamingo #Extreme

🚨 Caution trip hazard 🚨

These mussel-feeding oystercatcher chicks will learn vital feeding techniques from their parents for up to 26 weeks, one of the longest feeding apprenticeships of any wading bird.

#Earthcapture by @peanut.chu.adventures
.
.
.
.
#Oystercatcher #Birds #Wadingbirds

Manifesting life as an alpine frog #heatwave
 
As the ice begins to thaw, these common frogs emerge after spending months beneath the frozen surface. With only a brief alpine spring ahead, they have just enough time to breed before the cold returns. 
 
Celebrate 20 years of Planet Earth with us. Find out where to watch the full episode via our link in bio 💚

#PlanetEarth #DavidAttenborough #PE20 #PEIII

Ever seen a bear do this before? 🐻
 
As winter gives way to spring, bears emerge from hibernation and begin shedding their thick winter coats. You might spot them rubbing and scratching against trees. This not only helps with shedding but also leaves their scent behind, signalling to other bears who’s in the area.
 
Celebrate 20 years of Planet Earth with us. Find out where to watch the full episode via our link in bio 💚

#PlanetEarth #DavidAttenborough #PE20 #PEII

Behind the lens with Dan O’Neill 🎥 (Part 4/4)
 
Join wildlife biologist, broadcaster and filmmaker Dan as he answers questions about Tiger Island, from remarkable new tiger behaviours, to what it was like filming in Nepal.
 
See unseen footage from Tiger Island and watch the full Q&A via the link in bio 🔗
 
#TigerIsland #TigerCubs #BehindTheScene #Nepal

Join wildlife biologist, broadcaster and filmmaker Dan as he answers questions about Tiger Island, from remarkable new tiger behaviours, to what it was like filming in Nepal.
